message:
test(fcp-conformance/control-plane-retention): pin retention_for_schema classifier (br-91jd2)

retention_for_schema is the NORMATIVE classifier in fcp-protocol that
decides whether a control-plane object MUST be stored for audit
(Required) or MAY be dropped after processing (Ephemeral). Storage
operators rely on this single function to decide durability
obligations across every protocol message. Zero conformance coverage
despite documented invariants and a non-obvious dot-delimited
match defense.

Adds 14 tests against the public fcp_protocol API:

- 5 Required-prefix classifications (invoke/receipt/approval/audit/
  revoke — the audit-critical messages)
- 3 Ephemeral-prefix classifications (health/heartbeat/handshake)
- unknown_namespace_defaults_to_required_failsafe (the audit-
  failsafe — connectors publishing new schemas before the classifier
  knows about them MUST still be stored)
- pathological_namespace_does_not_match_ephemeral_prefix (the
  dot-delimited matcher prevents 'fcp.heartbeat-evil' from sneaking
  out of audit retention by mimicking 'fcp.heartbeat')
- legitimate_subnamespace_inherits_parent_retention (e.g.
  'fcp.heartbeat.v2' DOES inherit Ephemeral; 'fcp.audit.v3' DOES
  inherit Required)
- classification_is_independent_of_schema_name_and_version (only
  namespace is consulted)
- requires_storage_is_consistent_with_retention_for_schema (the
  two helpers must agree across every namespace)
- empty_namespace_defaults_to_required

Mid-stream Rust ergonomics fix: SchemaId::new takes impl Into<String>
so iterating over &[&str] needs an explicit deref of the loop var.

All 14 pass locally in <0.01s via cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test
control_plane_retention_classification_conformance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
